tax: Geode Latticeling is placed at species rank beneath Latticeling (Crystalline order, Earth Elementals class). Diagnosis rests on a hollow faceted quartz shell, directed crystal growth as attack, and a resonant ringing core. It is separated from its sibling the Selenite Bladeling by its hard ringing quartz shell versus the Bladeling's soft cleaving gypsum blades; the two share the genus body-plan but never the cleavage softness. Composition, not colour or menace, fixes the placement: interlocking quartz and amethyst grown on a self-templating lattice.
ap: Geode Latticeling stands 0.8–1.4 m, a clustered geode that walks on crystal stilts. Its silhouette is a faceted hollow ball bristling with point-terminated crystals. Three diagnostic surface markers separate it in the field: a clear bell-like ring when struck, from its hollow core; internal light refraction that throws lamp-glints in scattered points; and fresh blade-bright crystal at any recent break, regrown within minutes. It moves with a tinkling stilt-walk on grown crystal legs that snap and regrow as it moves, and shows no native light but intense refraction that splinters any lamp into a constellation. Even after dispersal it can be identified from a litter of shed perfect crystals and scratched glassy grooves on the floor, so a survey team need not see the living body to log the species.
eco: Geode Latticeling persists in vug-lined crystal galleries and quartz seams of Tier II, where silica-rich seep keeps its lattice fed. It does not breed; it forms where silica-saturated seep crystallises around a pressure node into a self-templating shell, and recurs whenever the seep re-saturates the vug and the lattice re-nucleates. It sustains its mass on silica supersaturation in seep water, drawing in dissolved silica drawn from seep, regrown as shell and blades. Range is bounded by the silica-rich seep zone: outside that band the body stops regrowing and is slowly worn to loose crystal. Juvenile or nascent forms appear as a vug whose crystals point the wrong way, against the usual inward growth, which lets a surveyor predict where an adult will rise.
beh: Behaviour is the behaviour of its medium given intent. Geode Latticeling is roused by a sharp sound or strike, which its core resonates to and otherwise lies indistinguishable from a natural crystal cluster. It claims a single crystal gallery and answers intrusion with growing blades outward to impale and ringing to disorient. Toward grey-folk it is reactive to noise; survey crews report that Forgemasters crave its shed crystals but learn to work the gallery in silence. It is not cultural and keeps no craft — its 'decisions' are thresholds of pressure, heat, current, or charge, not plans.
traitsText: Stable characters of Geode Latticeling: a body of interlocking quartz and amethyst grown on a self-templating lattice; immune to slashing and piercing, which only chip regrowing facets; and a defining weakness — a sustained resonant tone that shatters its hollow shell. Its senses run through the medium itself: acoustic resonance through its core; effectively deaf to anything that does not vibrate it. It tolerates heat and acid seep but fails at a matched resonant frequency, which fractures it. Mass varies with available element, yet the proportions, the hollow ringing shell and point-terminated crystals, stay constant, which is why mass alone never reclassifies the taxon.
abilities: Abilities of Geode Latticeling are bounded by its anatomy, not borrowed from other elements: it grows blade-sharp crystal outward to impale at arm's reach; it rings a disorienting tone from its hollow core; it regrows broken facets within minutes from seep silica; and it refracts light to dazzle and confuse aim. Each is a direct expression of self-templating silica crystallisation around a resonant void, so it cannot, for instance, produce flame, cross dry ground, or strike beyond blade reach. The powers scale with body mass and fade as the body disperses.
bg: In the Throat, Geode Latticeling matters because its galleries are prized for crystal yet kill the careless, and crews now work them in silence to avoid resonating the core. Grey-folk survey practice treats it as a noise-triggered hazard logged with crystal seams, and its presence reshapes routes through the Tier II crystal galleries, now worked quietly and shored against shard-growth. A vug of wrong-pointing crystals is the Forgemasters' warning that a Latticeling is nucleating.
g: Geode Latticeling (Crystallus geodicus) is a crystalline elemental that grows as a hollow geode-shell of interlocking quartz and strikes with shard-growth. Documented from the vug-pocketed crystal galleries of Tier II, it expresses a single inner element as its whole anatomy — a faceted shell of clear and amethyst quartz around a hollow resonant core — rather than as flesh laid over an animal skeleton. It builds and sheds blade-sharp crystal faster than any lithic kin can fuse stone. Field teams record it as a true elemental, not a beast wearing mineral armour: when it disperses it leaves a scatter of perfect loose crystals and a cracked hollow shell and no carcass.
